questions.text: The shortest distance measured from the initial to the final position of an object is known as the:
questions.explanation: Displacement is a vector quantity representing the straight-line distance between start and end points.

questions.text: An object travels 16m in 4s and then another 16m in 2s. What is the average speed of the object?
questions.explanation: Average speed = Total distance / Total time = (16+16) / (4+2) = 32/6 = 5.33 m/s.

questions.text: The rate of change of velocity is called:
questions.explanation: Acceleration is the measure of how quickly velocity changes over time.

questions.text: What does the slope of a distance-time graph represent?
questions.explanation: The slope (change in distance / change in time) of a distance-time graph gives the speed.

questions.text: A car starts from rest and attains a velocity of 18 km/h in 5 seconds. Its acceleration is:
questions.explanation: 18 km/h = 5 m/s. Acceleration = (5 - 0) / 5 = 1 m/s².

questions.text: If an object moves in a circular path with uniform speed, its motion is:
questions.explanation: Even if speed is constant, the direction changes continuously, making it an accelerated motion.

questions.text: Which of the following can be zero even when an object is in motion?
questions.explanation: If an object returns to its starting point, its displacement is zero.

questions.text: The area under a velocity-time graph represents:
questions.explanation: The area under the v-t curve equals the magnitude of displacement.

questions.text: Which equation of motion relates velocity and displacement?
questions.explanation: The third equation of motion connects acceleration, displacement, and initial/final velocities.

questions.text: The SI unit of acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Acceleration is velocity (m/s) divided by time (s), resulting in m/s².

questions.text: A physical quantity that has both magnitude and direction is called a:
questions.explanation: Vectors like displacement, velocity, and force require direction for complete description.

questions.text: The numerical ratio of displacement to distance for a moving object is:
questions.explanation: Displacement is the shortest path; it can be equal to distance (straight line) or less than it.

questions.text: Which instrument in a vehicle measures the total distance travelled?
questions.explanation: The odometer tracks cumulative distance, while the speedometer shows instantaneous speed.

questions.text: If an object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time, it is said to be in:
questions.explanation: Uniform motion implies a constant speed in a fixed direction.

questions.text: A car travels at 36 km/h. Its speed in metres per second (m/s) is:
questions.explanation: Multiply km/h by 5/18 to get m/s. 36 * (5/18) = 10 m/s.

questions.text: The slope of a velocity-time graph for an object provides its:
questions.explanation: Acceleration is defined as the rate of change of velocity, which is the slope of the v-t graph.

questions.text: Negative acceleration is also known as:
questions.explanation: Retardation or deceleration occurs when the velocity of an object decreases over time.

questions.text: If a body is moving in a circular path with constant speed, its velocity is:
questions.explanation: Velocity is a vector; since the direction changes constantly in a circle, velocity changes.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct second equation of motion?
questions.explanation: This equation relates displacement, initial velocity, time, and uniform acceleration.

questions.text: The area under a speed-time graph represents:
questions.explanation: Just as area under v-t graph is displacement, area under speed-time is distance.

questions.text: In uniform circular motion, the acceleration is directed:
questions.explanation: This is called centripetal acceleration, which acts towards the center of the path.

questions.text: An object starts from rest and moves with acceleration 2 m/s². Its velocity after 10s is:
When a distance-time graph is a straight line parallel to the time axis, the object is:
questions.explanation: A horizontal line on an s-t graph means distance isn't changing with time.

questions.text: The average velocity of a body is zero when its:
questions.explanation: Avg Velocity = Total Displacement / Total Time. If displacement is zero, avg velocity is zero.

questions.text: A stone is thrown vertically upwards and returns to the hand. Its total displacement is:
questions.explanation: Since the initial and final positions are the same, the displacement is zero.

questions.text: If an object moves at 20 m/s and comes to rest in 5s, its acceleration is:
Which graph represents uniform acceleration?
questions.explanation: Constant slope on a velocity-time graph indicates constant (uniform) acceleration.

questions.text: The speed of a body at a specific point in time is called:
questions.explanation: Instantaneous speed is what the speedometer of a car shows.

questions.text: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.explanation: Speed is distance divided by time, measured in metres per second.

questions.text: A athlete completes one round of a circular track of radius R in 40s. Displacement after 2 min 20s is:
questions.explanation: 140s = 3.5 rounds. After 3.5 rounds, the athlete is at the opposite end of the diameter.

questions.text: Can a body have constant speed and still be accelerating?
questions.explanation: In circular motion, speed is constant but direction changes, causing acceleration.

questions.text: If the v-t graph is parallel to the time axis, the acceleration is:
questions.explanation: A horizontal v-t line means velocity is not changing, so acceleration is zero.

questions.text: Velocity is defined as ________ per unit time.
questions.explanation: Velocity = Displacement / Time, whereas Speed = Distance / Time.

questions.text: The slope of the distance-time graph for a body at rest is:
questions.explanation: An object at rest has zero speed, so the slope of its s-t graph is zero.

questions.text: A train 100m long crosses a bridge 1km long at 72 km/h. Time taken to cross is:
questions.explanation: Total distance = 100+1000 = 1100m. Speed = 20 m/s. Time = 1100/20 = 55s.

questions.text: Which equation of motion is independent of displacement?
questions.explanation: The first equation (v=u+at) involves only velocity, time, and acceleration.

questions.text: Distance is a ________ quantity.
questions.explanation: Distance only has magnitude and no direction.

questions.text: When an object is thrown upwards, its acceleration at the highest point is:
questions.explanation: Gravity (9.8 m/s²) acts on the object throughout its flight, including at the top.

questions.text: The magnitude of the average velocity is equal to average speed when:
options: Motion is in a straight line
questions.explanation: In one-dimensional motion without reversal, distance equals displacement.

questions.text: Motion of a fan blade is an example of:
questions.explanation: Points on the blade travel in circles around the center of rotation.
